message:
feat(agent): improve parser accuracy and add NeedsDoubleEnter agent trait

Multiple improvements to the agent detection and parsing subsystem:

Pattern matching optimization:
- matchAny() and collectMatches() now skip empty pattern slices early
  and assume patterns are already lowercase, removing redundant
  strings.ToLower() calls on every pattern for each invocation.

Parser refactor:
- Replace free function collectMatches() with method countMatches() on
  parserImpl for pattern frequency detection. The new method counts
  unique pattern matches rather than returning a slice of matches,
  which is all the caller needed (len was immediately taken). This
  avoids slice allocations in the hot detection path.
- Replace inline tail-slicing of cleanOutput with util.SafeSliceFromEnd
  for RawSample extraction, ensuring rune-safe truncation.
- Replace all getLastNLines() calls with util.GetLastNLines(), removing
  the local implementation that was duplicated in patterns.go.

ANSI stripping enhancement:
- stripANSICodes() now handles carriage returns (\r) by simulating
  terminal overwrite behavior: for each line, only the text after the
  last \r is kept. This prevents false pattern matches on overwritten
  spinner/progress text that the terminal would never actually display.

New agent trait:
- AgentType.NeedsDoubleEnter() returns true for Codex and Gemini agents,
  which require a double-Enter sequence due to internal TUI buffering.
  This centralizes logic that was previously duplicated in
  swarm/prompt_injector.go's needsDoubleEnter() free function.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
